resources.string: Do you want to mark previous episodes as watched?
Easily find your favorite movies and series!\nFlux automatically organizes your videos to provide you with a simple and enjoyable library to use!
To display and play your videos, Flux needs access to your device\'s files.\nDon\'t worry, we do not collect or share any personal data.
To get started, make sure your video files are properly named. This allows the app to organize them automatically.
Don’t worry, it’s super easy! To keep your videos well organized, here’s how to name your files:
Give your movies a clear name, followed by the year if you want. For example:
For TV show episodes, use a format that includes the season and episode number. For example:

I\'ve been passionate about smartphones since my teenage years, and I decided to become an Android developer, a job I\'ve been doing for a few years now.\nBeyond that, I\'m just an average guy who loves manga, traveling, and Real Madrid.

Sometimes, you end up with a lot of video files and wish for an app to organize them simply. An app like Infuse exists on iOS, but since there\'s no equivalent on Android, I decided to create it myself for my personal needs.\nPlus, as an Android developer, this app also helps me stay up-to-date with the latest developments and serves as a kind of \"sandbox\".

This app will scan your video files longer than 5 minutes and extract information from the file name (title, season, episode, year). Then, the TMDB API is used to fetch additional information like the synopsis, rating, genre, etc.

This app is entirely developed in Kotlin with Jetpack Compose. I use Room for the database, Retrofit for network calls, and Hilt for dependency injection. I use also GitHub. As for the UI/UX, I use Material 3, and I did my best. Not too shabby, right?
How to contact me or suggest improvements?
If it\'s positive feedback, you can contact me by %1$s! Otherwise, do the same thing or directly on %2$s, but with as much kindness as if it were positive.

The goal is to keep improving the app, whether it\'s the UI, UX, or performance. I plan to retrieve more datas (genre, casting, etc) and I also plan to add new data sources, not just files on the device
To function, this application uses %1$s services. This site allows the retrieval of various information (images, titles, summaries, etc.) for your media. To use this service, an API key is required.
However, in order to be a completely FOSS (Free and Open-Source Software) application and ensure better control over your data, the API key for the TMDB service is not provided.

It is possible to continue without using the TMDB service; however, to take full advantage of the application\'s features, it is recommended to provide an API key. To do so, please follow these steps:
Retrieve your %1$s and copy it into the field below.
There are no items to display
Add your key to enjoy a better experience
